query: Josey Scott and Ian Watkins were both promising musicians. Which of these talented men was incarcerated, impacting his career with a rock band?When ready, output the final answer enclosed in <answer> and </answer> tags. Do not generate any content after the </answer> tag.
answer: Lostprophets disbanded in 2013 after Watkins was charged with sexual offences in late 2012.
query_analysis:
Summary of the Query:
Determine which of Josey Scott and Ian Watkins, both talented musicians, was incarcerated and how this impacted their careers in a rock band.

Skills and Tools:
1. **Research Skills**:
   - **Explanation**: To find information about Josey Scott and Ian Watkins, their careers, and any incidents of incarceration, research skills are essential.
2. **Web Search Capabilities**:
   - **Tools**: Ground_Google_Search_Tool, Web_RAG_Search_Tool, Wikipedia_RAG_Search_Tool
   - **Explanation**: These tools help gather information from various sources including the web, Wikipedia, and specific websites to find details about the musicians and their careers.

Additional Considerations:
- The query requires finding specific information about the musicians' careers and potential incarceration. Therefore, cross-referencing multiple sources is crucial to ensure accuracy.
- Using a combination of tools like Ground_Google_Search_Tool to find general information and Wikipedia_RAG_Search_Tool for more structured data from Wikipedia can provide a comprehensive answer.

[truncated]'}]}]}, 'Action Step 2': {'tool_name': 'Ground_Google_Search_Tool', 'sub_goal': 'Conduct a web search to find specific details about Josey Scott and Ian Watkins, focusing on any incarcerations and how it affected their careers in a rock band.', 'command': 'execution = tool.execute(query="Josey Scott and Ian Watkins incarcerations and career impact")', 'result': ['**Ian Watkins: Incarceration and Profound Career Destruction**

Ian Watkins, the former lead singer of the Welsh rock band Lostprophets, was incarcerated in December 2013 for a litany of severe sexual offenses involving children, including the attempted rape of a baby. He pleaded guilty to 13 charges, including sexual assault of children and possession of child and animal sexual abuse material.[1](https://www.walesonline.co.uk/news/wales-news/who-ian-watkins-lostprophets-32659632), [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ian_Watkins) Justice John Royce sentenced Watkins to 29 years in prison, with eligibility for parole in 2031 after serving two-thirds of his term, followed by six years of supervised release.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I) In 2019, he received an additional 10-month sentence for possessing a mobile phone in prison.[1](https://www.walesonline.co.uk/news/wales-news/who-ian-watkins-lostprophets-32659632)

Watkins' incarceration was marked by violence. He was transferred between various prisons, including HM Prison Wakefield and HM Prison Long Lartin.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I) In August 2023, he was reportedly taken hostage and stabbed by three inmates at HM Prison Wakefield, though his injuries were not life-threatening at the time.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I), [4](https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/crime/ian-watkins-dead-prison-lostprophets-singer-paedophile-b2843781.html), [5](https://www.hellomagazine.com/news/861059/lostprophets-singer-ian-watkins-cause-of-death-prison-attack/) Tragically, Watkins died on October 11, 2025, at HM Prison Wakefield after being attacked and suffering a fatal stab wound to the neck.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I), [1](https://www.walesonline.co.uk/news/wales-news/who-ian-watkins-lostprophets-32659632), [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ian_Watkins), [5](https://www.hellomagazine.com/news/861059/lostprophets-singer-ian-watkins-cause-of-death-prison-attack/) Two men have been charged with his murder.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I)

The impact on his career was immediate and catastrophic. Lostprophets, a band that had achieved significant commercial success with millions of albums sold worldwide and multiple top 10 UK albums, disbanded shortly before his trial in 2013.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I), [1](https://www.walesonline.co.uk/news/wales-news/who-ian-watkins-lostprophets-32659632), [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ian_Watkins), [4](https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/crime/ian-watkins-dead-prison-lostprophets-singer-paedophile-b2843781.html), [5](https://www.hellomagazine.com/news/861059/lostprophets-singer-ian-watkins-cause-of-death-prison-attack/), [6](https://www.dailystar.co.uk/news/latest-news/i-signed-lost-prophets-disgraced-33487487) The band's association with Watkins' heinous crimes irrevocably ended their collective career.

**Josey Scott: Personal Struggles and Career Redirection**

Josey Scott, best known as the former lead vocalist of the American rock band Saliva, has not been incarcerated. His career impact stems from his voluntary departure from Saliva in 2011 to address personal struggles with drug and alcohol addiction.[7](https://churchleaders.com/news/503443-former-saliva-singer-josey-scott-holy-spirit-wifes-prayers-saving-life.html) Scott openly credited his wife's support and his faith for helping him overcome these addictions, stating he "was going to die" if he had remained on his previous path.[7](https://churchleaders.com/news/503443-former-saliva-singer-josey-scott-holy-spirit-wifes-prayers-saving-life.html)

Following his departure, a legal dispute arose concerning the use of the band's name.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I), [8](https://blabbermouth.net/news/original-saliva-singer-josey-scott-accuses-bands-current-frontman-bobby-amaru-of-talking-out-of-both-sides-of-his-neck), [9](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GLrOz7hQdA4&vl=en) Scott, who founded Saliva in 1996 and contributed to their biggest hits, believes the name rightfully belongs to him.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I), [8](https://blabbermouth.net/news/original-saliva-singer-josey-scott-accuses-bands-current-frontman-bobby-amaru-of-talking-out-of-both-sides-of-his-neck) However, the remaining members of Saliva continued to perform under the name, citing a contractual agreement and the wishes of late guitarist Wayne Swinny.[8](https://blabbermouth.net/news/original-saliva-singer-josey-scott-accuses-bands-current-frontman-bobby-amaru-of-talking-out-of-both-sides-of-his-neck), [9](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GLrOz7hQdA4&vl=en) This has led to an ongoing feud and public disagreement with the current lineup of Saliva, particularly with the new frontman Bobby Amaru.[8](https://blabbermouth.net/news/original-saliva-singer-josey-scott-accuses-bands-current-frontman-bobby-amaru-of-talking-out-of-both-sides-of-his-neck), [9](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GLrOz7hQdA4&vl=en), [10](https://www.theprp.com/2025/10/05/news/josey-scott-savagely-goes-after-saliva-vocalist-bobby-amaru-on-a-pair-of-new-diss-tracks/)

After a hiatus, Josey Scott returned to music in 2024, touring with a new band and performing under the moniker "Josey Scott - The Original Voice of Saliva."[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I), [11](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Josey_Scott), [7](https://churchleaders.com/news/503443-former-saliva-singer-josey-scott-holy-spirit-wifes-prayers-saving-life.html) This rebranding was a resolution to the trademark dispute, allowing him to celebrate his legacy while distinguishing his solo endeavors from the continuing Saliva band.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I)']}}

result:
**Ian Watkins: Incarceration and Profound Career Destruction**

Ian Watkins, the former lead singer of the Welsh rock band Lostprophets, was incarcerated in December 2013 for a litany of severe sexual offenses involving children, including the attempted rape of a baby. He pleaded guilty to 13 charges, including sexual assault of children and possession of child and animal sexual abuse material.[1](https://www.walesonline.co.uk/news/wales-news/who-ian-watkins-lostprophets-32659632), [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ian_Watkins) Justice John Royce sentenced Watkins to 29 years in prison, with eligibility for parole in 2031 after serving two-thirds of his term, followed by six years of supervised release.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I) In 2019, he received an additional 10-month sentence for possessing a mobile phone in prison.[1](https://www.walesonline.co.uk/news/wales-news/who-ian-watkins-lostprophets-32659632)

Watkins' incarceration was marked by violence. He was transferred between various prisons, including HM Prison Wakefield and HM Prison Long Lartin.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I) In August 2023, he was reportedly taken hostage and stabbed by three inmates at HM Prison Wakefield, though his injuries were not life-threatening at the time.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I), [4](https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/crime/ian-watkins-dead-prison-lostprophets-singer-paedophile-b2843781.html), [5](https://www.hellomagazine.com/news/861059/lostprophets-singer-ian-watkins-cause-of-death-prison-attack/) Tragically, Watkins died on October 11, 2025, at HM Prison Wakefield after being attacked and suffering a fatal stab wound to the neck.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I), [1](https://www.walesonline.co.uk/news/wales-news/who-ian-watkins-lostprophets-32659632), [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ian_Watkins), [5](https://www.hellomagazine.com/news/861059/lostprophets-singer-ian-watkins-cause-of-death-prison-attack/) Two men have been charged with his murder.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I)

The impact on his career was immediate and catastrophic. Lostprophets, a band that had achieved significant commercial success with millions of albums sold worldwide and multiple top 10 UK albums, disbanded shortly before his trial in 2013.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I), [1](https://www.walesonline.co.uk/news/wales-news/who-ian-watkins-lostprophets-32659632), [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ian_Watkins), [4](https://www.independent.co.uk/news/uk/crime/ian-watkins-dead-prison-lostprophets-singer-paedophile-b2843781.html), [5](https://www.hellomagazine.com/news/861059/lostprophets-singer-ian-watkins-cause-of-death-prison-attack/), [6](https://www.dailystar.co.uk/news/latest-news/i-signed-lost-prophets-disgraced-33487487) The band's association with Watkins' heinous crimes irrevocably ended their collective career.

**Josey Scott: Personal Struggles and Career Redirection**

Josey Scott, best known as the former lead vocalist of the American rock band Saliva, has not been incarcerated. His career impact stems from his voluntary departure from Saliva in 2011 to address personal struggles with drug and alcohol addiction.[7](https://churchleaders.com/news/503443-former-saliva-singer-josey-scott-holy-spirit-wifes-prayers-saving-life.html) Scott openly credited his wife's support and his faith for helping him overcome these addictions, stating he "was going to die" if he had remained on his previous path.[7](https://churchleaders.com/news/503443-former-saliva-singer-josey-scott-holy-spirit-wifes-prayers-saving-life.html)

Following his departure, a legal dispute arose concerning the use of the band's name.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I), [8](https://blabbermouth.net/news/original-saliva-singer-josey-scott-accuses-bands-current-frontman-bobby-amaru-of-talking-out-of-both-sides-of-his-neck), [9](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GLrOz7hQdA4&vl=en) Scott, who founded Saliva in 1996 and contributed to their biggest hits, believes the name rightfully belongs to him.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I), [8](https://blabbermouth.net/news/original-saliva-singer-josey-scott-accuses-bands-current-frontman-bobby-amaru-of-talking-out-of-both-sides-of-his-neck) However, the remaining members of Saliva continued to perform under the name, citing a contractual agreement and the wishes of late guitarist Wayne Swinny.[8](https://blabbermouth.net/news/original-saliva-singer-josey-scott-accuses-bands-current-frontman-bobby-amaru-of-talking-out-of-both-sides-of-his-neck), [9](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GLrOz7hQdA4&vl=en) This has led to an ongoing feud and public disagreement with the current lineup of Saliva, particularly with the new frontman Bobby Amaru.[8](https://blabbermouth.net/news/original-saliva-singer-josey-scott-accuses-bands-current-frontman-bobby-amaru-of-talking-out-of-both-sides-of-his-neck), [9](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GLrOz7hQdA4&vl=en), [10](https://www.theprp.com/2025/10/05/news/josey-scott-savagely-goes-after-saliva-vocalist-bobby-amaru-on-a-pair-of-new-diss-tracks/)

After a hiatus, Josey Scott returned to music in 2024, touring with a new band and performing under the moniker "Josey Scott - The Original Voice of Saliva."[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I), [11](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Josey_Scott), [7](https://churchleaders.com/news/503443-former-saliva-singer-josey-scott-holy-spirit-wifes-prayers-saving-life.html) This rebranding was a resolution to the trademark dispute, allowing him to celebrate his legacy while distinguishing his solo endeavors from the continuing Saliva band.[3](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EkSHYUkBV2I)
